references.title: Predicting at-risk students in the early stage of a blended learning course via machine learning using limited data

extracted.Courses: 1 Spring Semester Course and a 1 Fall Semester Course
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors draw from SRL models to create 5 of the variables used. 

Authors use SMOTE. Data is always Aggregated from moment 1 to moment 7.
extracted.Comments:
Authors do 2 different works:

1 - A Benchmark study which, across different periods does a holdout separation using the entire sample. 

2 - A prospective study which usus the 2018 version of the course as trainig and the 2019 as test data.

The comparison between RF-Benchmark and RF-Prospective shows that performance is better across the board. Authors use SHAP to understand the importance of the features and find study in time as being particularly relevant.
extracted.Limitations:
- Small sample size

- Does not consider other contexts - too course specific

- Only uses RF

- Does not consider additional data sources

annotations.text: Work tries to dientify students at risk in a blended learning course with limited data. The authors compare 2 approaches:
The authors extract and categorize multiple motivational variables.
Authors found prospective study to be in line with the benchmark study. SHAP emphaiszes importance of time management variables for early identification of students at risk.
